evidence.scientific_clarity: Forest plot clearly shows 12 per-study CIs with weight-proportional squares, diamond pooled estimate, null line, and favour labels — brief's scientific claim is fully conveyed.
evidence.typography: pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype=none set in rcParams; pdffonts confirms CID TrueType (Arial/Arial-Italic), no Type-3 bitmap fonts; font sizes 7–9 pt are legible.
evidence.palette: Single-hue blue (#0F4D92) for studies, near-black for pooled diamond, grey for null line — no red/green used, no categorical confusion; colorblind-safe.
evidence.layout_density: figsize=(7,6) with tight_layout; y-range padded just 0.7 units beyond data; extra x-margin reserved for pooled annotation; no overlapping labels visible.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content exactly (12 studies, fixed-effects, pooled log OR = 0.245 [0.196, 0.294]); favour labels present; no legend needed (single series).
evidence.vector_fidelity: pdffonts shows embedded subsetted CID TrueType fonts (Arial); no Type-3 raster fonts; script also verifies this at runtime with fallback raw-bytes check.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (35 KB), figure.png (107 KB), caption.tex, gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial; data.json not listed but no grade.json yet — note: figure.svg also produced (bonus), but data.json absence is minor.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states the finding with specific numbers (pooled log OR = 0.245, 95% CI [0.196, 0.294]), explains diamond, square-size encoding, dashed null line, and N=12.

overall_strengths: Fully vector PDF with embedded Arial (CID TrueType, fonttype=42) verified programmatically at generation time — no bitmap font risk.
Caption is publication-ready: quantitative finding stated explicitly, all visual encodings explained, matches figure exactly.
overall_weaknesses: data.json is not present in the output directory (only referenced by the script), so the figure is not fully self-contained/reproducible from the delivered files alone.
No figure.svg is required by the brief, but the script saves one anyway — minor scope creep; more importantly, the PNG lacks a companion data.json for archival reproducibility.
